meaning: vt.发誓放弃,避免,公开放弃
examples: We repent, we abjure, we will break from our chain.
GEOFF PULLUM really doesn't like people who abjure the passive voice.
To realize this goal, in two ways, all artists had to abjure their isolation from the society.
Markets may (and just did) correct for overvalue, but they can't abjure the laws of physics.
But the same deafness afflicts the same people when they are exhorted to abjure other sorts of unhealthy behaviour.
